contentBlocks.translation: Names 1–10: Oṃ Bhadrakāḷyai namaḥ (the auspicious Kāḷī) | Oṃ Kāmarūpāyai namaḥ (she of desired forms) | Oṃ Mahāvidyāyai namaḥ (the great wisdom) | Oṃ Yaśasvinyai namaḥ (the glorious) | Oṃ Mahāśrayāyai namaḥ (the great refuge) | Oṃ Mahābhāgāyai namaḥ (the supremely blessed) | Oṃ Dakṣayāgavibhēdinyai namaḥ (who disrupted Dakṣa's sacrifice) | Oṃ Rudrakōpasamudbhūtāyai namaḥ (born from Rudra's fury) | Oṃ Bhadrāyai namaḥ (the auspicious) | Oṃ Mudrāyai namaḥ (the sacred seal) — 10.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 11–20: Oṃ Śivaṅkaryai namaḥ (who causes auspiciousness) | Oṃ Chandrikāyai namaḥ (radiant as moonlight) | Oṃ Chandravadanāyai namaḥ (moon-faced) | Oṃ Rōṣatāmrākṣaśōbhinyai namaḥ (beautiful with eyes red with fury) | Oṃ Indrādidamanyai namaḥ (subduer of Indra and the gods) | Oṃ Śāntāyai namaḥ (the peaceful) | Oṃ Chandralēkhāvibhūṣitāyai namaḥ (adorned with the crescent moon) | Oṃ Bhaktārtihāriṇyai namaḥ (remover of devotees' suffering) | Oṃ Muktāyai namaḥ (the liberated) | Oṃ Chaṇḍikānandadāyinyai namaḥ (who bestows joy on Chaṇḍikā's devotees) — 20.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 21–30: Oṃ Saudāminyai namaḥ (lightning-like) | Oṃ Sudhāmūrtyai namaḥ (whose form is nectar) | Oṃ Divyālaṅkārabhūṣitāyai namaḥ (adorned with divine ornaments) | Oṃ Suvāsinyai namaḥ (sweet-scented) | Oṃ Sunāsāyai namaḥ (of beautiful features) | Oṃ Trikālajñāyai namaḥ (knower of three times) | Oṃ Dhurandharāyai namaḥ (bearer of burdens) — 27 | Oṃ Sarvajñāyai namaḥ (all-knowing) | Oṃ Sarvalōkēśyai namaḥ (ruler of all worlds) | Oṃ Dēvayōnayē namaḥ (of divine origin) — 30.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 31–40: Oṃ Ayōnijāyai namaḥ (self-manifest, not womb-born) | Oṃ Nirguṇāyai namaḥ (beyond the three attributes) | Oṃ Nirahaṅkārāyai namaḥ (without ego) | Oṃ Lōkakaḷyāṇakāriṇyai namaḥ (doer of good for all the world) | Oṃ Sarvalōkapriyāyai namaḥ (dear to all the worlds) | Oṃ Gauryai namaḥ (the golden-fair) | Oṃ Sarvagarvavimardinyai namaḥ (crusher of all pride) | Oṃ Tējōvatyai namaḥ (full of radiant power) | Oṃ Mahāmātrē namaḥ (the Great Mother) | Oṃ Kōṭisūryasamaprabhāyai namaḥ (radiant as ten million suns) — 40.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 41–50: Oṃ Vīrabhadrakṛtānandabhōginyai namaḥ (who shares Vīrabhadra's bliss) | Oṃ Vīrasēvitāyai namaḥ (served by heroes) | Oṃ Nāradādimunistutyāyai namaḥ (praised by Nārada and the sages) | Oṃ Nityāyai namaḥ (the eternal) | Oṃ Satyāyai namaḥ (truth itself) | Oṃ Tapasvinyai namaḥ (the great ascetic) | Oṃ Jñānarūpāyai namaḥ (whose form is knowledge) | Oṃ Kaḷātītāyai namaḥ (beyond all measure) | Oṃ Bhaktābhīṣṭaphalapradāyai namaḥ (granter of devotees' desired fruits) | Oṃ Kailāsanilayāyai namaḥ (dwelling on Kailāsa) — 50.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 61–70: Oṃ Dakṣagarvāpahāriṇyai namaḥ (remover of Dakṣa's pride) | Oṃ Śivaśāsanakartryai namaḥ (executor of Śiva's command) | Oṃ Śaivānandavidhāyinyai namaḥ (who bestows joy on Śiva's devotees) | Oṃ Bhavapāśanihantryai namaḥ (destroyer of worldly bondage) | Oṃ Savanāṅgasukāriṇyai namaḥ (who blesses the sacred ritual) | Oṃ Lambōdaryai namaḥ (the vast-wombed) | Oṃ Mahākāḷyai namaḥ (the great dark Kāḷī) | Oṃ Bhīṣaṇāsyāyai namaḥ (of terrifying visage) | Oṃ Surēśvaryai namaḥ (queen of the gods) | Oṃ Mahānidrāyai namaḥ (the great cosmic sleep) — 70.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 71–90: Oṃ Yōganidrāyai namaḥ (the yogic sleep) | Oṃ Prajñāyai namaḥ (transcendent wisdom) | Oṃ Vārtāyai namaḥ (the sustaining medium) | Oṃ Kriyāvatyai namaḥ (filled with activity) | Oṃ Putrapautrapradāyai namaḥ (granter of sons and grandsons) | Oṃ Sādhvyai namaḥ (the virtuous) | Oṃ Sēnāyuddhasukāṅkṣiṇyai namaḥ (who delights in battle) | Oṃ Śambhōḥ IcChāyai namaḥ (Śiva's will-power) | Oṃ Kṛpāsindhavē namaḥ (ocean of compassion) | Oṃ Chaṇḍyai namaḥ (the fierce) — 80 | Oṃ Chaṇḍaparākramāyai namaḥ (of terrifying valor) | Oṃ Śōbhāyai namaḥ (splendor) | Oṃ Bhagavatyai namaḥ (the divine Goddess) | Oṃ Māyāyai namaḥ (the world-weaving power) | Oṃ Durgāyai namaḥ (the inaccessible) | Oṃ Nīlāyai namaḥ (the dark-blue) | Oṃ Manōgatyai namaḥ (swift as thought) | Oṃ Khēcharyai namaḥ (sky-wanderer) | Oṃ Khaḍginyai namaḥ (bearing a sword) | Oṃ Chakrahastāyai namaḥ (holding the discus) — 90.

contentBlocks.translation: Names 91–100: Oṃ Śūlavidhāriṇyai namaḥ (wielding the trident) | Oṃ Subāṇāyai namaḥ (bearing fine arrows) | Oṃ Śaktihastāyai namaḥ (with the spear in hand) | Oṃ Pādasañchāriṇyai namaḥ (who moves with divine grace) | Oṃ Parāyai namaḥ (the supreme) | Oṃ Tapaḥsiddhipradāyai namaḥ (granter of ascetic perfection) | Oṃ Dēvyai namaḥ (the Goddess) | Oṃ Vīrabhadrasahāyinyai namaḥ (companion of Vīrabhadra) | Oṃ Dhanadhānyakaryai namaḥ (creator of wealth and grain) | Oṃ Viśvāyai namaḥ (the universal) — 100.

ōṃ manōmālinyahāriṇyai namaḥ ।
ōṃ sunakṣatrōdbhavakaryai namaḥ ।
ōṃ vaṃśavṛddhipradāyinyai namaḥ ।
ōṃ brahmādisurasaṃsēvyāyai namaḥ ।
ōṃ śāṅkaryai namaḥ ।

ōṃ pratyakṣaparamēśvaryai namaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Names 101–108 (and closing names): Oṃ Manōmālinyahāriṇyai namaḥ (remover of mental impurity) | Oṃ Sunakṣatrōdbhavakaryai namaḥ (who brings auspicious births) | Oṃ Vaṃśavṛddhipradāyinyai namaḥ (bestower of dynastic increase) | Oṃ Brahmādisurasaṃsēvyāyai namaḥ (served by Brahmā and the gods) | Oṃ Śāṅkaryai namaḥ (she of Śaṅkara) | Oṃ Priyabhāṣiṇyai namaḥ (sweet of speech) | Oṃ Bhūtaprētapiśāchādihāriṇyai namaḥ (remover of evil spirits) | Oṃ Sumanasvinyai namaḥ (noble-hearted) | Oṃ Puṇyakṣētrakṛtāvāsāyai namaḥ (who dwells at holy pilgrimage sites) | Oṃ Pratyakṣaparamēśvaryai namaḥ (Supreme Goddess who is directly perceptible).
Thus ends the Śrī Bhadrakāḷī Aṣṭōttaraśatanāmāvaḷi (the Garland of One Hundred and Eight Names of Śrī Bhadrakāḷī).
